metadata.description: Comprehensive repair knowledge base for Jack's 2004 Jaguar X-Type (X400 platform, Australian-spec, RHD). Designed for LM context injection in the Drifter AI mechanic assistant.
metadata.coverage: Engine, Transmission, Electrical, CAN bus, Chassis, Suspension, Brakes, Steering, Body, Climate, Security, Lighting, Audio, Diagnostics, Maintenance

2.0L_petrol.ecm_location: Engine compartment, front bulkhead, RH side

2.5L_V6.ecm_location: Engine compartment, front bulkhead, RH side

3.0L_V6.ecm_location: Engine compartment, front bulkhead, RH side

2.0L_diesel.ecm_location: Engine compartment, front bulkhead, RH side

automatic.towing_limit: Maximum 0.5 miles at 30 mph with front wheels on ground

manual.towing_limit: Maximum 30 miles at 30 mph with front wheels on ground

drivetrain.type: All-Wheel Drive (AWD) standard on V6 models

abbreviations.CMP: Camshaft Position Sensor (RH Bank /1, LH Bank /2)

ECM_V6.location: Engine compartment, front bulkhead, RH side

Restraints_Module.location: Center of vehicle, behind trim panel

engine_compartment_fuse_box.location: Left side of engine compartment, adjacent to battery

relays_gasoline.R5: Power Distribution Fuse Box Ignition Relay

relays.R5: Passenger Junction Fuse Box Ignition Relay

G14.location: Rearward of Power Distribution Fuse Box

G17.location: On generator bracket, RH side of engine

G36.location: LH side of cross car beam, behind instrument panel

G37.location: RH side of cross car beam, behind instrument panel

G38.location: Roof, top of LH E-post (sedan only)

G1.location: Trunk, LH rear (under LH tail lamp unit)

wheel_alignment.measurement_conditions: All fluids full, full fuel tank, tires at normal pressure. Ride height measured from wheel center to apex of wheel arch.

ride_height.measurement_note: Showroom condition: vehicle empty, 9 litres fuel in tank

steps: Start engine and centralize steering wheel
Turn engine off, attach rigid link from steering wheel to brake pedal
Check toe setting using four wheel alignment equipment
Loosen tie rod end locknuts
Reposition steering gear boot outer clamps
Rotate tie-rods equal amount clockwise or counterclockwise to adjust
Tighten tie-rod end locknuts to 40 Nm
Reposition steering gear boot outer clamps
Recheck toe setting
Check toe setting using four wheel alignment equipment
Raise and support vehicle
Loosen rear lower arm cam bolt nuts
Rotate cam bolts equal amounts to adjust toe
CAUTION: Final tightening must be with vehicle on its wheels
Lower vehicle
Bounce vehicle to settle suspension
Tighten rear lower arm cam bolt nuts to 115 Nm
Raise and support vehicle
Ensure wheel rotates freely and brake pads retracted
Hold tire at top and bottom, move inward/outward while lifting weight off bearing
If loose or does not rotate freely, install new bearing (front: 204-01, rear: 204-02)

maintenance_schedule.overview: Scheduled maintenance intervals in kilometers and miles

items: Fluid level checks (coolant, brake fluid, washer fluid)

periodic_items.item: Accessory drive belt inspection and renewal
periodic_items.notes: Inspect at earlier intervals, replace as needed

diagnostic_trouble_codes.overview: Complete EOBD/OBD2 diagnostic codes for Jaguar X-Type powertrain systems

dtc_codes.description: Mass Air Flow (MAF) Sensor out of range

dtc_codes.default_action: ECM Default - Default air mass used, Adaptive fuel metering inhibited, Canister purge inhibited, Maximum engine speed reduced

dtc_codes.description: Transmission control system malfunction

additional_codes_p0763_p0878.monitoring_conditions: Comprehensive component monitor transmission drive cycle
additional_codes_p0763_p0878.default_action: TCM Default - If short circuit to B+ V - TCC permanently engaged, If other cause - TCC inoperative
possible_causes: Transmission to TCM TCC solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal TCC solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit

additional_codes_p0763_p0878.description: Line pressure control solenoid circuit malfunction
possible_causes: Transmission to TCM line pressure solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal line pressure solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit

additional_codes_p0763_p0878.monitoring_conditions: Comprehensive component monitor transmission drive cycle
additional_codes_p0763_p0878.default_action: TCM Default - If short circuit to B+ V - fixed 5th gear. If other cause - fixed 4th gear at high vehicle speed, fixed 5th gear at high vehicle speed
possible_causes: Transmission to TCM shift solenoid A circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal shift solenoid A circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Shift solenoid A pressure solenoid failure

additional_codes_p0763_p0878.monitoring_conditions: Comprehensive component monitor transmission drive cycle
additional_codes_p0763_p0878.default_action: TCM Default - If short circuit to B+ V - fixed 3rd gear, If other cause: fixed 4th gear
possible_causes: Transmission to TCM shift solenoid B circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal shift solenoid B circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Shift solenoid B pressure solenoid failure

additional_codes_p0763_p0878.monitoring_conditions: Comprehensive component monitor transmission drive cycle
possible_causes: Transmission to TCM shift solenoid C circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal shift solenoid C circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Shift solenoid C pressure solenoid failure
Transmission to TCM 2/4 brake pressure solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit
Transmission internal 2/4 brake pressure solenoid circuit: open circuit, short circuit

additional_emseobd_codes_p0791_p1346.default_action: ECM Default - Speed control inhibited, Maximum throttle opening for N range inhibited, Throttle opening limited to 30%, Maximum engine speed reduced

additional_emseobd_codes_p0791_p1346.description: 2nd Gear signal to TCM circuit: short circuit, open circuit, high resistance, 3rd Gear signal to TCM circuit: short circuit, open circuit, high resistance, J Gate ground circuit: open circuit, high resistance

transmission_dtc.note: See powertrain_ems section for TRANS/EOBD codes and TCM-specific DTCs

coolant.renewal_interval: Every 5 years or 240,000 km (150,000 miles), whichever comes first

climate_control.zones: Single or dual zone (varies by trim)

security_system.central_locking: Electronic with remote key fob and manual override

key_positions.OFF: Engine off, all systems powered down
key_positions.ON: Full electrical power, ready to start

security_system.alarm: Audible and visual alerts on unauthorized access

jump_starting.battery_location: Under hood, left side of engine compartment

towing.automatic_transmission: Maximum 0.5 miles at 30 mph with front wheels on ground
towing.manual_transmission: Maximum 30 miles at 30 mph with front wheels on ground
towing.recommendation: Professional recovery recommended for longer distances

emergency_procedures.inertia_switch: Fuel pump cut-off on impact - reset by pressing button

08_exterior_lighting: 08.1-08.10 (10 variants covering sedan/wagon, auto/manual headlamps, trailer towing, HID leveling)
16_telematics: 16.1-16.6 (Phone ROW/NAS, Voice Control, Navigation, Japan Nav)
19_ancillaries: 19.1 Horn, Cigar Lighter, Accessory Connectors, Garage Door Opener, Electrochromic Mirror

monitor_drive_cycles.oxygen_sensor: Warm engine, drive at steady speed 40-60 mph for 5+ minutes
monitor_drive_cycles.fuel_system: Drive at various speeds and loads for 10+ minutes
monitor_drive_cycles.misfire: Drive at various RPM ranges including idle, cruise, and acceleration
monitor_drive_cycles.catalyst: Drive at steady 55 mph for 3+ minutes after full warmup
monitor_drive_cycles.evaporative_purge: Drive at steady cruise after cold start for 10+ minutes

elm327_notes.ble_connection: Web Bluetooth API via BLE ELM327 adapter

workshop_manual_sections.note: Complete workshop manual covers 415+ sections. Key section codes below.

lm_usage_guide.description: Instructions for how the Drifter LM should use this knowledge base
search_tips: For DTC lookups: search diagnostic_trouble_codes.systems.powertrain_ems.dtc_codes by code field
For electrical faults: check control_modules for pin assignments, then ground_points for return paths, then fuses_and_relays for protection
For CAN bus issues: reference electrical_system.networks.CAN for node architecture and pin locations
For maintenance questions: check maintenance_schedule for intervals and fluids_and_capacities for specs
For alignment/suspension: reference chassis_and_suspension for specs and procedures with torque values
Use specific technical terms (e.g., 'P0171 lean fault') rather than generic queries for best results
Cross-reference wiring_diagrams_index to locate the relevant figure number for any electrical system
For specific misfire diagnosis: reference known_issues_and_troubleshooting.P0303_cylinder_3_misfire for a complete step-by-step workflow
For Australian-specific questions: check australian_specification for RHD differences, fuel specs, VicRoads RWC requirements, and local parts sourcing
diagnostic_workflow: 2. Look up code in diagnostic_trouble_codes for description, possible causes, and default actions
3. Identify affected control module and its pin assignments in electrical_system.control_modules
4. Check relevant fuse and relay in fuses_and_relays
5. Verify ground point integrity using ground_points
6. Reference wiring_diagrams_index for the relevant figure number
7. Use live OBD-II PIDs to verify sensor readings against expected values
8. Follow repair procedure from workshop_manual_sections if applicable

australian_specification.description: Key differences between Australian-delivered (ROW) X-Types and NAS (North American) models

regulatory.emissions: ADR 79/04 (equivalent to Euro III for 2004 MY)
regulatory.obd_standard: EOBD (European On-Board Diagnostics) — same protocol as NAS OBD-II but different readiness monitor thresholds
regulatory.vin_format: SAJWA01Axx_xxxxxx where position 4 = body (W=sedan), position 5 = engine (A=2.5L, B=3.0L), position 8 = model year

electrical_differences.drive_side: RHD — wiper park position, instrument cluster, and headlamp beam pattern are mirror-image of LHD
electrical_differences.headlamps: RHD beam pattern (dipped to the left for left-side traffic). Headlamp leveling wiring and ground points follow RHD variant paths (G10AR, G37BL for RHD)
electrical_differences.lighting: Front and rear turn signals use amber lenses (not red/amber combo as NAS)
electrical_differences.fog_lamps: Rear fog lamp fitted as standard (single, LH side) — not standard on NAS
electrical_differences.daytime_running_lamps: Not required under ADR — may not be fitted
electrical_differences.instruments: Speedometer in km/h (primary), odometer in km
electrical_differences.radio_frequency: AM/FM tuned for Australian broadcast bands

mechanical_differences.fuel: Unleaded petrol 95 RON minimum (PULP). Australian fuel standards differ from US — ethanol blends (E10) may be used but E85 is NOT compatible
mechanical_differences.cooling: Same cooling system as all markets but Australian ambient temperatures may stress the system more
mechanical_differences.air_conditioning: R134a refrigerant, same system — may cycle more frequently in Australian heat
mechanical_differences.tyre_pressures: Same specifications as global model. Common Australian OEM tyre sizes: 205/55 R16, 215/45 R17, 225/40 R18
mechanical_differences.spare_wheel: Space-saver spare (T125/85 R16) or full-size depending on trim

victoria.rwc_required: Roadworthy Certificate (RWC) required for vehicle transfer in Victoria
inspection_items: Tyres (minimum 1.5mm tread depth — stricter than some states)
Brakes (pad thickness, disc condition, brake fluid)
Lights (all external lights functional, correct beam pattern)
Windscreen and wipers (no cracks in driver's line of sight)
Exhaust system (no leaks, emissions within limits)
common_xtype_rwc_failures: Front lower control arm bushings worn (very common on X-Types)
Power steering fluid leaks at pump or rack seals
Front suspension spring breakage (known X-Type weak point)
CV boot splits on front halfshafts
Headlamp beam alignment (especially after headlamp bulb replacement)

parts_sourcing_australia.oem_alternatives: Ford Mondeo Mk3 / Ford Contour parts cross-reference for many chassis and drivetrain components
parts_sourcing_australia.spark_plug_spec: NGK ITR6F-13 (iridium, AJ-V6) or Motorcraft AGSF32FM
parts_sourcing_australia.coolant_spec: Jaguar OAT coolant or equivalent long-life OAT (orange). Do NOT mix with IAT (green) coolant

P0303_cylinder_3_misfire.severity: HIGH — triggers limp mode (ECM Default: maximum engine speed reduced)
P0303_cylinder_3_misfire.ecm_default_action: Maximum engine speed reduced. If catalytic converter damage risk detected, CHECK ENGINE light will flash.

cylinder_location.bank: Bank 1 (RH side of engine, looking from front)
cylinder_location.cylinder_numbering_diagram: FRONT OF ENGINE: [2,1] / [4,3] / [6,5] (LH bank = 2,4,6 / RH bank = 1,3,5)
cylinder_location.note: There is NO 'Bank 3' on a V6. Bank 1 = RH cylinders (1,3,5). Bank 2 = LH cylinders (2,4,6).

P0303_cylinder_3_misfire.ignition_coil: Individual coil-on-plug (COP) unit for cylinder 3, RH bank middle position

step_1_verify_spark_plug_installation.description: Confirm plugs are correct spec and properly installed
checks: Correct plug type: NGK ITR6F-13 (iridium) or Motorcraft AGSF32FM
Gap: 1.0-1.1mm (0.039-0.043 in) — DO NOT regap iridium plugs
Torque: 11-15 Nm (hand-tight plus 1/4 turn if no torque wrench). Over-torquing can crack the porcelain insulator
Check for cross-threading (would cause compression loss)
Anti-seize NOT recommended on iridium plugs (changes torque spec)

step_2_check_coil_pack.description: Most common cause of persistent misfire after plug change on AJ-V6
checks: Remove coil from cylinder 3 and inspect boot for carbon tracking (black lines/burns inside the rubber boot)
Check coil connector is fully seated — a partially clicked connector causes intermittent misfire
Look for cracked coil housing (dropping the coil during plug change can crack it internally)

SWAP TEST: Move cylinder 3 coil to cylinder 1 and cylinder 1 coil to cylinder 3. Clear DTCs and drive. If the misfire follows the coil (now P0301), the coil is faulty. If it stays P0303, the coil is fine.
step_2_check_coil_pack.common_problem: If you accidentally pulled the coil by the wiring rather than the connector body, internal winding damage can occur. The coil may work at idle but fail under load.

step_3_check_what_you_may_have_disturbed.description: Things commonly damaged or dislodged during spark plug replacement on the AJ-V6
checks: Vacuum hoses on intake manifold — pulling on the engine cover or reaching across can dislodge small vacuum lines, causing a lean misfire
Fuel injector connector on cylinder 3 — the injector connectors sit close to the coil wells and can be accidentally unclipped
Coil connector wiring — if the loom was pulled tight, check for broken wires at the connector
Debris in spark plug well — if debris fell into the well during plug change, it can short the coil to ground
PCV valve hose (runs across top of engine) — easily knocked off, causes rough idle and lean codes

step_4_check_injector_circuit.description: If coil swap test passes, check fuel delivery to cylinder 3
checks: Listen for injector click at cylinder 3 using a mechanic's stethoscope or long screwdriver to ear
Measure injector resistance: should be 12-16 ohms across pins
Check ECM pin EN16-092 for continuity to injector connector
Check for 12V at injector connector with ignition ON (one wire will show battery voltage)
NOID light test: insert NOID light into injector connector and crank — light should pulse

step_5_compression_test.description: If spark and fuel are confirmed good, check mechanical condition
checks: Perform compression test on all 6 cylinders — specification is 12-14 bar (174-203 psi)
Cylinder 3 should be within 10% of the highest reading
If low: perform wet test (add tablespoon of oil) — if compression rises, rings are worn. If no change, valves may be the issue.
Check for coolant in oil or oil in coolant (head gasket failure symptoms)

step_6_live_data_via_obd.description: Use Drifter OBD-II connection to read live data

pids_to_monitor.misfire_counters: Mode 06 TID $A3-$A8 (per-cylinder misfire counters if supported)
pids_to_monitor.fuel_trims: Short Term Fuel Trim Bank 1 (PID 0x06) and Long Term Fuel Trim Bank 1 (PID 0x07) — values above +15% suggest lean condition on that bank
pids_to_monitor.o2_sensors: Bank 1 Sensor 1 (PID 0x14) — should oscillate 0.1-0.9V at cruise. Flat at 0.45V = dead sensor
pids_to_monitor.rpm_stability: Engine RPM (PID 0x0C) — look for rpm dips/fluctuations at idle indicating misfire events
pids_to_monitor.coolant_temp: PID 0x05 — ensure engine reaches operating temp (85-95°C). If not, thermostat may be stuck open, causing enrichment issues

things_that_make_it_worse.description: Common mistakes that escalate a cylinder 3 misfire on the AJ-V6

scenarios.action: Driving with active misfire for extended periods
scenarios.consequence: Unburnt fuel washes cylinder walls (dilutes oil on that cylinder), damages catalytic converter. Raw fuel entering the cat can cause it to overheat and melt the substrate.

scenarios.action: Repeatedly clearing DTCs and driving without fixing root cause
scenarios.consequence: ECM adaptive fuel trims become increasingly skewed. Long-term fuel trim can go so far out of range that even after fixing the misfire, the engine runs poorly until trims re-learn (can take 50-100 miles of driving or a manual reset).

scenarios.action: Swapping plugs between cylinders after one was fouled
scenarios.consequence: A fouled plug from a misfiring cylinder can foul a good cylinder. Always use fresh plugs.
Can crack the plug insulator (causing intermittent misfire), strip the aluminium head threads (loss of compression), or crush the sealing washer too flat.

scenarios.action: Using wrong plug type or gap
scenarios.consequence: AJ-V6 requires fine-wire iridium plugs with precise gap. Standard copper plugs will misfire under load. Pre-gapped plugs should not be re-gapped.

limp_mode_details.trigger: ECM detects sustained misfire (typically 2+ consecutive drive cycles with active misfire)
symptoms: Rev limiter set much lower than normal
CHECK ENGINE light on solid or flashing
limp_mode_details.recovery: Fix root cause, clear DTCs with scan tool, perform drive cycle to complete OBD monitors. Limp mode clears when ECM confirms no misfire for a full drive cycle.
limp_mode_details.warning: If CHECK ENGINE light is FLASHING (not steady), stop driving immediately — this indicates active misfire severe enough to damage the catalytic converter.

common_xtype_v6_issues.frequency: Very common — all 6 coils are the same part and can be swapped for diagnosis
common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Misfire under load, rough idle, DTC P0301-P0306
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Replace failed coil(s). Consider replacing all 6 at the same service if mileage is high.

common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Rough idle, stalling, P0222/P0223 TP sensor codes, limp mode
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Clean throttle body first. If fault persists, replace unit and perform throttle adaptation reset via scan tool.

common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Poor acceleration, lean codes (P0171/P0174), rough running
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Clean with MAF-specific cleaner (not carb cleaner). Replace if cleaning doesn't resolve.

common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Knocking over bumps, vague steering, uneven tyre wear, RWC failure
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Replace lower arm assemblies (bushings not separately serviceable on most variants)

common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Whining noise on steering, low fluid, power steering fluid on ground
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Replace seals or pump assembly. Check fluid cooler hose connections.

common_xtype_v6_issues.issue: Rear differential mount bushing failure (AWD models)
common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Clunk from rear on acceleration/deceleration, vibration

common_xtype_v6_issues.frequency: Moderate — often caused by prolonged misfires
common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: P0420/P0430 catalyst efficiency below threshold, reduced power, sulphur smell
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Replace catalytic converter. Always fix the root cause (misfire, rich running) before replacing cat.

common_xtype_v6_issues.symptoms: Engine slow to warm up, heater blows cold, slightly rich fuel trims
common_xtype_v6_issues.fix: Replace thermostat assembly. The X-Type thermostat housing is a known failure point.
